description:
This image is a screenshot of a Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) user interface displaying a geographic map overlaid with an interactive rule evaluation panel. The interface is set for "Country: NL Netherlands/Nederland" and "Region: NL11 Groningen". The map shows a patchwork of land cover/land use types, with prominent areas in green, brown, red, and dark blue, indicative of an agricultural landscape. Place names visible on the map include "Munninkweg", "Mensumaweg", "Bakkerom", "Hoolweg", "Boerakker", "Hoofdweg", "Oostwold", and "Hoendiep". A scale bar indicates 500 m / 3000 ft. The map is credited to "Leaflet | © Core003 Mosaic, © OpenStreetMap contributors".

The central overlay panel, titled "The following table shows the result of all the rules of the selected output class," lists five evaluation rules. Each rule uses the `maxCov` function (indicating maximum coverage of matching input classes) with a specific Land Cover Class (LCC), Land Use Activity (LUA), or Land Cover Hierarchical (LCH) element, a barcode value of '5', and a threshold of 30.0%.
- Two rules evaluate to `True`:
    - `maxCov("LCC-herbaceous-vegetation", 5) >= 30.0`
    - `maxCov("LUA-commercial-crop-production", 5) >= 30.0`
- Three rules evaluate to `False`:
    - `maxCov("LCH-arable-crop-land", 5) >= 30.0`
    - `maxCov("LCH-crop-rotation", 5) >= 30.0`
    - `maxCov("LCH-no-irrigation", 5) >= 30.0`
The panel also includes a "PREVIEW" button.
